page: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor architecture is x86_64, supported by a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data reveals a mix of strong single-threaded capabilities and moderate multi-threaded performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance scores 1954.58, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression register strong results at 34.67 GB/sec and 14.51 GB/sec respectively. However, multi-threaded scaling is limited, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 4032.62 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 28.21 GB/sec, both falling into the weak category. Memory performance is similarly split; while large block uncached read bandwidth is average at 44.91 GB/sec, cached small block read bandwidth and large block write bandwidth are weak.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4s_v7 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The inclusion of advanced CPU instruction sets, such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX, provides hardware-level acceleration for cryptography and basic vector math, which is reflected in its average Passmark encryption score of 4585.96. However, the instance struggles with larger computational models, as demonstrated by its poor LLM text generation speed of 4.97 tokens/second on a medium 7B model. This server is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, lightweight background processing, and workloads requiring strong single-threaded CPU performance.
description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 34.67 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.51 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded and memory-write operations are relatively weak, as shown by a multi-core stress-ng score of 4032.62 and a write bandwidth of 22.85 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, aiding cryptography and vector operations. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for balanced workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, lightweight application hosting, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-threaded performance for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-threaded performance for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel processors, strong single-threaded execution, and hardware-accelerated cryptography.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs.
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and no local storage.
Strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression benchmarks.
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX.
Best suited for general-purpose computing, static web serving, and lightweight applications.
